name: error-screamer
description: Your code misbehaves, this extension screams. Plays error sounds on terminal failures, red squiggles, failed builds, broken saves, and debugger crashes — then celebrates when you finally get it right. Customizable sounds, escalation mode, funny toasts, Do Not Disturb schedule, waveform viewer, and per-sound volume/speed/pitch controls.

errorScreamer.enabled.description: Master switch — enable or disable the Error & Success Reactor extension entirely.

errorScreamer.muted.description: Quick mute — suppresses all sounds without disabling the extension (streak tracking and stats still run).

errorScreamer.activeSound.description: The sound ID (filename without .mp3) to play when a terminal command fails. File must be in sounds/errors/.

errorScreamer.successSound.description: Sound ID to play when a terminal command succeeds (exit code 0). File must be in sounds/success/. Leave empty to disable.

errorScreamer.successCooldownSeconds.description: Minimum seconds between success sound triggers. Prevents rapid-fire success sounds.

errorScreamer.randomErrorSound.description: When enabled, a random sound from the enabled error sounds library is picked on each error instead of the active sound.

errorScreamer.randomSuccessSound.description: When enabled, a random sound from the enabled success sounds library is picked on each success instead of the configured success sound.

errorScreamer.cooldownSeconds.description: Minimum number of seconds that must pass between sound triggers. Prevents rapid-fire spamming.

errorScreamer.ignoredExitCodes.description: List of exit codes that should never trigger the error sound (e.g. [1, 130] to ignore Ctrl+C and general errors).

errorScreamer.doNotDisturbEnabled.description: Enable the Do Not Disturb schedule to suppress sounds during certain hours.

errorScreamer.doNotDisturbStart.description: Do Not Disturb start time in 24-hour format (HH:MM). Sounds are suppressed from this time until doNotDisturbEnd.

errorScreamer.doNotDisturbEnd.description: Do Not Disturb end time in 24-hour format (HH:MM). Sounds resume after this time.

errorScreamer.escalationEnabled.description: When enabled, the sound automatically gets louder and faster as your error streak grows.

errorScreamer.escalationThreshold.description: Number of consecutive errors before escalation kicks in.

errorScreamer.escalationVolumeBoost.description: Volume added per escalation tier above the threshold.

errorScreamer.escalationSpeedBoost.description: Speed added per escalation tier above the threshold.

errorScreamer.errorPatternDetectionEnabl.description: When enabled, the extension also scans terminal output text for error patterns (see errorPatterns). Useful for tools that print errors but still exit with code 0. Requires VS Code 1.93+.

errorScreamer.errorPatterns.description: Text patterns to scan terminal output for when errorPatternDetectionEnabled is true. If any pattern matches, the error sound triggers even if the command exited with code 0.

errorScreamer.showErrorToast.description: Show a brief notification toast after each error sound plays, displaying the exit code and which sound was triggered.

errorScreamer.funnyToasts.description: When showErrorToast is enabled, use funny randomized messages instead of plain info toasts.

errorScreamer.playOnDiagnostics.description: Play the error sound when the number of Error-severity diagnostics (red squiggles) increases in the active file.

errorScreamer.playOnSave.description: Play the error sound when you save a file that still has Error-severity diagnostics.

errorScreamer.playOnTaskFailure.description: Play the error sound when a VS Code task process exits with a non-zero code.

errorScreamer.playOnDebuggerCrash.description: Play the error sound when a debug session terminates.

errorScreamer.diagnosticDebounceMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after diagnostics change before checking for new errors. Prevents rapid-fire triggers while typing.
